claims.evidence_text: SD showed prominent variation, varying between 1.34 and 4.14.
claims.claim_summary: Spike density in the tetraploid/hexaploid 2SY wheat RIL population varied from 1.34 to 4.14 spikelets per cm across environments.
claims.methods: Spike density computed as spikelet number per spike (SNS) divided by spike length (SL); five/three plants per line across multiple environments

claims.evidence_text: And a SD range from 1.65 to 3.85 was observed in AM RILs.
claims.claim_summary: Spike density in the tetraploid (durum) AM wheat RIL population (Ailanmai x wild emmer) ranged from 1.65 to 3.85 spikelets per cm.
organisms: Triticum turgidum (tetraploid/durum wheat, AM AL/LM001 RIL population)
claims.methods: Spike density computed as spikelet number per spike (SNS) divided by spike length (SL); five plants per line across eight environments

claims.claim_summary: BLUP-based mean spike density in the hexaploid 2SY wheat RIL population was about 2.14 spikelets per cm (range 1.70-2.80).
claims.methods: Best linear unbiased prediction (BLUP) across seven environments

claims.claim_summary: BLUP-based mean spike density in the tetraploid (durum) AM wheat RIL population was about 2.31 spikelets per cm (range 2.01-2.87).
organisms: Triticum turgidum (tetraploid/durum wheat, AM AL/LM001 RIL population)
claims.methods: Best linear unbiased prediction (BLUP) across eight environments
